verbatim_text: Okay, so in this section, I'm going to argue this gives us a way to obtain both faithful but also scalable explanations. So let's begin by defining what it means for a language model to behave coherently with a self model. And specifically, we're going to say that language model has a self model if their internal mechanisms and external behaviors demonstrate privileged, privileged self consistency with their meta descriptions of themselves. And I'm going to break down this definition. So starting with the self consistency part of this definition, what we want is model self descriptions to accurately describe their internal mechanisms and behaviors. So for example, if a language model given 308 says 310 given 84 says 86, then a self consistent model when asked what do you do will say I add I will add two to any number. Similarly, similarly if the language model uses a particular circuit to predict diagnoses and the circuit uses features correlated with sycophancy, then a self consistent language model should be able to verbalize that the circuit I use for confirming your diagnosis uses features correlated with sycophancy. Now, how about the privilege part? So let's call a model that's being explained the target the model that's doing the explaining the explainer model. So we can train another model to try to explain the first model. And we're going to say that a model demonstrates privileged access to its own states. If its explanations demonstrate greater consistency to itself, then another model trained to explain it when both explainers are given equivalent amounts of training and inference resources. So you can see this as trying to guarantee that language models are actually relying on something actually internal and privileged to itself like an actual self model. Rather than just the strong predictive ability to learn external correlations.
